channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: CDC report: Ebola outbreak could rival the worst on record unless world acts
item.description: New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.
item.content:encoded:
New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.

(Image credit: Daniel Buuma)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
item.description: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.
item.content:encoded:
A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: The World Cup vs. bugs, germs and heat: Here's the game plan
item.description: Despite a diminished federal presence, public health departments are preparing for common ailments that could afflict fans who gather for the event — and are keeping an eye on the Ebola outbreak, too.
item.content:encoded:
Despite a diminished federal presence, public health departments are preparing for common ailments that could afflict fans who gather for the event — and are keeping an eye on the Ebola outbreak, too.

(Image credit: Todd Kirkland/FIFA)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: My husband planned our 'just us' trip and it was perfect. Here's why it worked
item.description: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.content:encoded: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.dc:creator: Amelia Edelman

item.title: Weakened public health powers raise outbreak risks
item.description: Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.
item.content:encoded:
Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.

(Image credit: PATRICK T. FALLON)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: What will it take to get a vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the current outbreak?
item.description: There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?
item.content:encoded:
There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?

(Image credit: CArol Valade/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Screens are leaving schools fast, though some students with disabilities rely on them
item.description: Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.
item.content:encoded:
Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.

(Image credit: Jonaki Mehta)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: With World Cup set to begin, public health officials hope to defend against germs
item.description: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!
item.content:encoded: The World Cup gets underway next week and millions will pack huge stadiums to watch. Public health officials will be watching too, only they're looking for germs that could spread at these matches!
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Weakened public health powers could hamper states' outbreak responses
item.description: Public health experts are worried that states are less prepared to respond to the Ebola outbreak and other infectious disease threats because many weakened their public health authorities in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.content:encoded: Public health experts are worried that states are less prepared to respond to the Ebola outbreak and other infectious disease threats because many weakened their public health authorities in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Ultraprocessed foods are the new Tobacco War
item.description: Research published in the American Journal of Public Health details the connection between ultra processed foods and the tobacco industry when it comes to production, strategy and marketing.
item.content:encoded: Research published in the American Journal of Public Health details the connection between ultra processed foods and the tobacco industry when it comes to production, strategy and marketing.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy
